Now I have comprehensive information. Let me create a detailed report summarizing my findings: ## Investigation Report: Anchor Link Navigation Issue on Women's WOPT Pages Based on my thorough investigation of the codebase, I've identified the root cause of the anchor link issue and several contributing factors. Here are my findings: ### 1. QUIZ RESULT HTML STRUCTURE (Lines 232-246 in JS bundles) All four JS bundles render the quiz results with anchor links after completion: - **womens-hormone-imbalance-pmax.bundle.js** (lines 239, 242) - **womens-hormone-imbalance-tt.bundle.js** (lines 242, 245) - **womens-menopause.bundle.js** (lines 200, 203) - **womens-menopause-tt.bundle.js** (lines 200, 203) All contain the same anchor link: ```html I'm Ready To Start ``` **Finding:** The anchor links are correctly set to `#whats-included` and are properly rendered in the quiz result container. ### 2. ANCHOR TARGET LOCATION (page-womens-hormone-imbalance-pmax.php, line 234) The target `#whats-included` is correctly placed on a section element: ```php
``` **Finding:** The target anchor exists and is properly positioned in the page. ### 3. PAGE STRUCTURE & HEIGHT ISSUE (womens-hormone-imbalance.scss, lines 952-953) **CRITICAL FINDING - This is the root cause:** ```scss main { display: flex; flex-direction: column; height: 100%; min-height: 100vh; } ``` The `
` element has `min-height: 100vh` (100% of viewport height). This is set on line 952-953 of the SCSS file. This combined with `display: flex` means the main element will always be at least full viewport height. This affects the layout and potentially confuses browser anchor link navigation. ### 4. REVIEWS CAROUSEL STRUCTURE (page-womens-hormone-imbalance-pmax.php, lines 139-233) The reviews section (`.reviews-carousel-women`) contains: - Slick carousel initialized at line 273 of the JS bundle - Reviews carousel HTML at lines 212-226 of the PHP template with nested divs: - `.slick-slide` (outer) - `
` (middle wrapper) - `.action` with `tabindex="-1"` and `style="width:100%;display:inline-block"` - `.review.review-collapsed` **Important:** The `.action` div has `tabindex="-1"` which removes it from tab order but doesn't prevent clicks. ### 5. REVIEWS CAROUSEL CSS (womens-hormone-imbalance.scss, lines 1878-2089) Key CSS properties: - `.reviews-carousel-women` has padding and standard styling (no problematic overflow or height restrictions) - `.reviews-carousel-women .carousel` at line 1946 has no height constraints - Slick carousel responsive settings are properly configured (lines 273-295 of JS bundle) **Finding:** No CSS-level overflow or height issues on the reviews section itself. ### 6. QUIZ LONGFORM SECTION (womens-hormone-imbalance.scss, lines 3698-3847) The quiz-longform (listicle) section between the reviews and "what's-included": - Has padding: 2.5rem 0 (mobile), 3rem 0 (tablet), 5rem 0 (desktop) - No problematic height properties - Standard flex/grid layout with no overflow:hidden **Finding:** The listicle section doesn't have any height restrictions. ### 7. SLICK CAROUSEL BEHAVIOR Slick carousel initialization (womens-hormone-imbalance-pmax.bundle.js, lines 273-295): ```javascript $('.carousel').slick({ slidesToShow: 3, infinite: false, slidesToScroll: 1, dots: true, arrows: true, responsive: [ {breakpoint: 768, settings: {slidesToShow: 1, slidesToScroll: 1}}, {breakpoint: 1280, settings: {slidesToShow: 2, slidesToScroll: 1}} ] }); ``` **Finding:** Standard Slick configuration, no `draggable: true` option set (defaults to true on desktop). Slick's minified library contains `preventDefault()` calls for click handling. ### 8. BREAKPOINTS (layout.scss, lines 14-35) - `$small-screen: 600px` - `$medium-screen: 1024px` - `$large-screen: 1280px` These affect responsive behavior of the reviews carousel and other sections. ### SUMMARY OF FINDINGS **Root Cause:** The page layout issue is likely related to one or more of these factors: 1. **`main` element min-height: 100vh** (line 952-953 of SCSS) - This forces the main content area to be at least full viewport height, which can interfere with normal scroll-to-anchor behavior, especially on pages where content is shorter initially. 2. **Slick Carousel with `draggable: true` by default** - While click handling on links should work, Slick may be capturing mouse events on carousel items. The `.action` wrapper with `display: inline-block` and `tabindex="-1"` could be interfering. 3. **Review carousel height calculation** - While no explicit height is set, Slick dynamically calculates slide heights. On certain viewport sizes or with certain content, this could be causing layout shifts that make the browser scroll to the wrong anchor. 4. **Missing explicit scroll-to anchor JavaScript** - None of the quiz result links have event handlers. They rely entirely on native browser anchor navigation, which can be affected by dynamic content (the Slick carousel) and the full-viewport-height main element. ### RECOMMENDATIONS FOR INVESTIGATION 1. Test whether the anchor scrolls correctly on pages without a Slick carousel 2. Check if removing `min-height: 100vh` from `main` fixes the issue 3. Add JavaScript to handle anchor navigation after Slick initialization completes 4. Verify carousel height doesn't cause vertical scroll offset 5. Check if the issue is specific to certain viewport widths (responsive breakpoints)

On certain viewport sizes or with certain content, this could be causing layout shifts that make the browser scroll to the wrong anchor. 4. **Missing explicit scroll-to anchor JavaScript** - None of the quiz result links have event handlers. They rely entirely on native browser anchor navigation, which can be affected by dynamic content (the Slick carousel) and the full-viewport-height main element. ### RECOMMENDATIONS FOR INVESTIGATION 1.
